---
title: "The Outdoors"
---

---

I spend a lot of time thinking about the environment in an academic context. But, most of my most formative experiences with nature have nothing to do with lab deadlines or a GIS software. Here a some outdoor trips that have stuck with me.

---

### Colorado River Canoe Trip
###### October 2023 | UCSB Adventure Programs

![](images/Colorado4.JPG)

This one was equal parts adventure and professional development. I went with UCSB's Adventure Programs, a program I was actively training in my first year to become an outdoor trip leader myself. This weekend took us on a multi-day canoe trip down the Colorado River. We visited the Hoover Dam, soaked in hot springs along the way, and worked on paddling technique in moving water. The trip ended with a 6am push to get back to Santa Barbara in time for class, headlamps strapped on, canoeing in pure darkness. Easily one of the more surreal mornings of my life.

---

### Blackcomb Mountain, Whistler
###### September 2024 | Hiking Trip

![](images/BlackComb1.jpg){width=20%} ![](images/BlackComb2.jpg){width=36%} ![](images/BlackComb3.jpg){width=36%}

Took the gondola up to a trailhead and hiked from there. It was long, tiring, and at one point very questionably navigated... we briefly were lost. But! The views made it completely worth it. Whistler in September is busy at the base but quiet once you get high enough, and we had long stretches of trail essentially to ourselves. I'd give it a 4.5 out of 5 and easily go back up tomorrow.

---

### Olympic National Park Backpacking Trip
###### July 2025 | Brothers Trip

My twin brother and older brother and I backpacked into the Olympic backcountry and camped at Royal Lake. I can attest that it is indeed a "hidden gem." We lost the water filter somewhere along our trek up, so every drop of water got boiled over the stove. We survived, mostly because everyone had overpacked food by a wildly significant margin. The nights there were the quietest I've ever experienced. We played the guitar by a fire each night, swam in the lake, and we all felt that distance from our laptops. 

![](images/Backpack1.jpg){width=49%} ![](images/Backpack2.jpg){width=49%}

---
